description.value: Varian ARIA API Implementation Guide

valueMarkdown.value: Automatically added as a dependency - all IGs depend on HL7 Terminology
Automatically added as a dependency - all IGs depend on the HL7 Extension Pack

description.value: This resource identifies the healthcare documentation created for a Patient in ARIA
Performer of the Activity of Care Plan.
The predecessor activity this care plan activity is based on.
This resource allows for the definition of some activity to be performed, it may be used to specify actions to be taken as part of a workflow, appointment or tasks.
ActivityDefinition example for the *Varian ARIA API ActivityDefinition Profile.*
Classification of Activity.
Address Telephone 1 Number.

AllergyIntolerance represents Patient's Allergies in ARIA.
All recorded allergies or NO KNOWN ALLERGY status will be reflected using this.

This value set defines different allergy types configured in Aria. List of configured allergy categories are available under valueset 'aria-allergy-category'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/allergy-intolerance-category.
AllergyIntolerance example for the *Varian AllergyIntolerance API Group Profile.*
ARIA Coded Allergy category.
ARIA allergy reaction.
A booking of a healthcare event among patient(s), practitioner(s), related person(s) and/or device(s) for a specific date/time. This may result in one or more Encounter(s).
Appointment example for the *Varian ARIA API Appointment Profile.*
Actual Start date & Actual End date for Appointment.
ARIA Specific Status.
Visit instructions of a MO appointment
Visit instructions of a MO appointment
The date on which the Physical Exam record approved.
Date on which observation result value has been approved.
A record of an event made for purposes of maintaining a security log. Typical uses include detection of intrusion attempts and monitoring for inappropriate usage.
AuditEvent example for the *Varian ARIA API AuditEvent Profile.*
This resource record details about an anatomical structure. This resource may be used when a coded concept does not provide the necessary detail needed for the use case.
Cancer Condition example for the *Varian ARIA API Cancer Condition Profile.*
Care Plan profile is derived from FHIR Domain Resource. It represents care plan in ARIA .
CarePlan example for the *Varian ARIA API CarePlan Profile.*

The configured careplan template in Aria. List of configured templates are accessible using valueset 'aria-careplan-template'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/careplan-template
Base Activity Definition reference on which this Care Plan activity is planned.
Careplan activities auto Assign oncologist flag.

This Resource includes all the people and organizations who plan to participate in the coordination and delivery of care for a patient.
Careteam represents department and practitioner (doctor/staff) assigned to the Patient in ARIA.
ARIA does not configure predefined teams, so single Careteam per Patient will represent associations with Department and practitioner.
CareTeam example for the *Varian ARIA API CareTeam Profile.*
This Resource represents charges captured for an activity.
ChargeItem example for the *Varian ARIA API ChargeItem Profile.*
Medical charges with procedure.
Type of Charge
Code Modifier Override on base Procedure code.
Resource completed On.
Sub-component attribute name.
Comment on component of Observation.
Lab Result Component Order.
The date on which the existence of the Condition was first asserted or acknowledged.
ARIA Cancer Behaviour Code. It is used to uniquely identify an ICD-O behavior code.
The date on which symptoms of the diagnosis/problem were first known to exist.
ARIA Codes for Diagnosis Status.

This resource is used to record detailed information about a diagnosis that has risen to a level of concern. The condition could be a point in time diagnosis in context of an encounter, it could be an item on the practitioner's Problem List, or it could be a concern that doesn't exist on the practitioner's Problem List.
Indicates that the diagnosis was brought in via interface (i.e. it was done external to our application).
Designated codes for condition side of the body affected.
Concepts that identify the significance or priority of the diagnosis code. Note that the codes are numeric, and the number of the code represents the ordinal priority of the associated diagnosis.
Signifies if the observation is in woking group.

This value set defines a base set of codes for countries configured in Aria. The list of country accepted under citizenShip are available under ValueSet 'aria-country'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/country
Configurable Attributes on Patient. ARIA has Configurable User defined Attributes to accommodate Patient's properties which are not present in ARIA out of box.
Daily dose limit
The resource is default choice among similar set
Default Department of Resource.

This resource identifies an instance or a type of a manufactured item that is used in the provision of healthcare without being substantially changed through that activity. The device may be a medical or non-medical device. Medical devices include durable (reusable) medical equipment, implantable devices, as well as disposable equipment used for diagnostic, treatment, and research for healthcare and public health. Non-medical devices may include items such as a machine, cellphone, computer, application, etc.
Device example for the *Varian ARIA API Device Profile.*

List of configured device types are accessible using valueset 'aria-device-type'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/device-type

List of available codes are accessible using valueset 'aria-diagnosiscode'. All values can be accessed using ValueSet $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/condition-code. Codes can be filtered on schemas using context filter i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/condition-code&context=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-9-cm|icd-9-cm
Whether User confirmation dialog flag. Applicable: DIRECTIVE ValueSet only.
DocumentReference example for the *Varian ARIA API DocumentReference Profile.*

This valueset defines the code specifying the precise type of document. List of configured document type are accessible using valueset 'aria-documenttype'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/documentreference-type&publisher=Organization-Prov-1
Date at which document approved.
Whether the document draft has be completed for review or approval.
Location of document stored.
Document Signer details.
Originator of Document, In ARIA its Supervisor of document.
Document Template Name in ARIA.
Example breast cancer Condition.
An example female radiotherapy Patient.
An example radioterapy Practitioner.

Group represents logical grouping of Resources like Advance Practitioners, Nurses,Oncologists, System administrators etc... Group comprises Doctor/Staff (Practitioner), Machine (Device) and Venue (Location) with in a department in ARIA.
Group example for the *Varian ARIA API Group Profile.*

HealthcareService represents departments in ARIA as in ARIA department and services are not two separate concepts. Although Department are already being represented by Organization resource, since many other FHIR clinical resources works on HealthcareService concept, we are mapping department in Healthcare service as well.
HealthcareService example for the *Varian ARIA API HealthcareService Profile.*
Flag in ARIA to define charge capture after completion of a service in Facility.
Whether the ethnicity or race is primary? Applicable to Patient ethnicity and race.
Lab Result Component publishable status.
Is Current Stage is working stage.
Last modified by task name.
Last modified by user.
Last update time of Element.

Location includes both incidental locations (a place which is used for healthcare ) and dedicated, formally appointed locations. It can be of Auxiliary type or Venue type. Auxiliary locations include imaging machines and treatment machines. Venues are locations within a department like treatment room, waiting room, consultation room, etc. Some locations can be schedulable.
Location example for the *Varian ARIA API Location Profile.*
Associated department in ARIA (Supported only on ARIA V18 or onwards)

This value set includes various mobile phone providers configured in aria. The list of configured mobile phone providers are available under ValueSet 'aria-mobilephoneprovider'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/patient-mobilephoneprovider
Modifier application code.
Observation Condition Diagnosis Stage example with all attributes specified.
Observation Stage Category example with all attributes specified.

Observations are a central element in healthcare, used to support diagnosis, monitor progress, determine baselines and patterns and even capture demographic characteristics. Most observations are simple name/value pair assertions with some metadata, but some observations group other observations together logically, or even are multi-component observations.
This profile is used to represent the Stage Category in Condition Diagnosis resource.
Stage Classification, clinical, pathological, posttherapy, recurrence, autopsy is called the Classification.

Observations are a central element in healthcare, used to support diagnosis, monitor progress, determine baselines and patterns and even capture demographic characteristics. Most observations are simple name/value pair assertions with some metadata, but some observations group other observations together logically, or even are multi-component observations.
This profile is used to represent the Stage Group in Condition Diagnosis resource.
Observation-vital example for the *Varian ARIA API Observation-vital Profile.*

Vital Signs profile is derived from FHIR Observation.
It represents Patient's Vital Signs Observations recorded in ARIA .

Vital Signs component profile is derived from FHIR Observation.
It represents Patient's individual Vital Signs result Observations recorded in ARIA .
Lag offset from previous activity.

A formally or informally recognized grouping of organizations formed for the purpose of achieving some form of collective action. Includes institutions, departments etc
Organization represents Hospital and Departments configured in ARIA. A Hospital can have multiple departments.
Hospital is represented with category 'prov (Provider)' and Department is represented with category 'dept (Department)'.
Hospital and Department relationship is represented on Department.
Organization Department example for the *Varian ARIA API Organization Department Profile.*
Organization Hospital example for the *Varian ARIA API Organization Hospital Profile.*

This profile represents Patient Demographics, Contacts and other administrative information. Profile Contains: Demographics, Contacts, Region specific details like Race, Ethnicity and Language (if applicable), User Defined Labels
Patient CareTeam Bundle create example with all attributes specified.
Patient Citizenship Value Set

List of configured death reasons values are available under valueset 'aria-patient-deathreason'. All database configured values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/patient-deathreason

This value set includes patient ethnicity configured in aria. The list of configured ethnicity are available under ValueSet 'aria-patient-ethnicity'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/ethnicity
Patient example for the *Varian ARIA API Patient Profile.*

The gender of a person used for administrative purposes. All database configured values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/person-gender

This value set includes supported gender identity configured in aria. The list of configured gender identity are available under ValueSet 'aria-patient-genderidentity'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/patient-genderIdentity

This value set includes configured patient ids. The list of country accepted under citizenShip are available under ValueSet 'aria-country'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/country

This value set defines the set of codes that can be used to indicate patient's communication language. The list of configured communication languages are available under ValueSet 'aria-patient-language'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/languages

This value set includes Marital Status values configured in aria. The list of configured marital status are available under ValueSet 'aria-patient-maritalstatus'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/marital-status

This value set includes race configured in aria. The list of configured races are available under ValueSet 'aria-patient-race'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/race

This value set includes religion configured in aria. The list of configured religion values are available under ValueSet 'aria-patient-religion'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/religion

This value set includes supported sexual orientation configured in aria. The list of configured sexual orientation are available under ValueSet 'aria-patient-sexorientation'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/patient-sexorientation

This value set includes user defined labels defined for Patient resource. The list of configured labels are available under ValueSet 'aria-patient-userdefinedlabel'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/patient-userdefinedlabel

This value set includes Patient Status values configured in aria. The list of configured patient status are available under ValueSet 'aria-patient-status'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/patient-status
Patient's class - In Patient | Out Patient.
Whether the contact is primary.
Patient's relationship with the contact person.
Reason for Patient's Death.
Whether Patient is test patient. (Supported only on ARIA V18 or onwards) FHIR will return test patient which are created by ARIA. Test Patient Create/update is not supported from FHIR.
In Patient Admission details - Admission Period, Room Number.
Patient's mobile phone provider details.
Patient Transportation Contact Comment.
Patient's occupation details.
Patient's photo type.
Patient's retirement details contains information about patient's retirement date, retirement reason and retirement note.
Patient's status with provider - New Patient | Previous Patient.
Age when procedure is performed.
Person's Gender, if is not re-presentable with Administrative Gender Value set.

Practitioner covers all individuals who are engaged in the healthcare process and healthcare-related services as part of their formal responsibilities and this Resource is used for attribution of activities and responsibilities to these individuals. Practitioners include (but are not limited to):
physicians, dentists, pharmacists, physician assistants, nurses, scribes, midwives, dietitians, therapists, optometrists, paramedics, medical technicians, laboratory scientists, prosthetic technicians, radiographers, social workers, professional homecare providers, official volunteers, receptionists handling patient registration
Practitioner represents Doctor and Staff configured in ARIA.
Doctor/Staff demographics, role and association with department is being represented.
Practitioner example for the *Varian ARIA API Practitioner Profile.*

List of practitioner ids configured are available under valueset 'aria-practitioner-ids'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/practitioner-ids

The different practitioner roles configured are available under valueset 'aria-practitioner-role'. All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/practitioner-role
Bolus used (or to be used) for radiotherapy.

The elements of a treatment course that can be defined during prescription. This represents the treatment course in ARIA, not an actual prescription and is the parent for all presciptions in the course. It contains the data elements of a course that are not specific to planning or delivery, for example, treatment intent, relation to the diagnosis, or course status. Those elements are exposed as CodeX RT Radiotherapy Course Prescription although ARIA does not have an actual prescription for the complete course. See the Phase Prescription profile for actual prescriptions in ARIA. With the Radiotherapy Course Prescription, elements of the course can be found as soon as a course is defined in ARIA, even if no treatment plans or delivered treatments are available yet.
A summary of the treatment progress in a radiotherapy course of treatment. When additional treatments are delivered in the same course, this resource is updated. No new resource is created.
Example radiotherapy patient.

A prescription for a radiotherapy treatment phase. In ARIA, a prescription can cover a single plan or complete phase implemented with one or multiple plans. During prescription it may not even be defined whether multiple plans will be used. Therefore, all ARIA prescriptions are exposed as Phase Prescriptions.
A summary of all plans in a radiotherapy course of treatment. Only approved plans with status other than Unapproved, Rejected, Reviewed are considered.

A summary of all plans in a radiotherapy treatment phase. In ARIA, all plans linked to the same prescription are considered as part of the same phase. One Radiotherapy Planned Phase is exposed for each ARIA prescription that has at least one linked plan in an approved status (other than Unapproved, Rejected, Reviewed).
Radiotherapy Prescription Energy
Radiotherapy Prescription Energy
Radiotherapy Prescription Modality
Radiotherapy Prescription Modality
Radiotherapy Prescription Site
Radiotherapy Prescription Site
Radiotherapy Prescription Technique.
Radiotherapy Prescription Technique.
A summary of the treatment progress in a radiotherapy treatment phase. In ARIA, all plans linked to the same prescription are considered as part of the same phase.
A summary of the treatment progress in one radiotherapy treatment plan.
A radiotherapy treatment plan.

A volume of the body used in radiotherapy planning or treatment delivery. In ARIA, dose is specified and tracked against Reference Points. These Reference Points can represent actual points or three-dimensional volumes. ARIA calls all of those 'Reference Point', even if not a point. Each Reference Point in ARIA is exposed as a Radiotherapy Volume.
Example BodyStructure compliant to the RadiotherapyVolume profile.
Radiotherapy Course Prescription example from Codex RTTD collection. Included because referenced by the Radiotherapy Phase Prescription.
Example Radiotherapy Course Prescription, i.e. parent to all prescriptions in the Course.
Example Radiotherapy Course Summary.
Radiotherapy Phase Prescription example from Codex RTTD collection.
Example Radiotherapy Phase Prescription.
Example Planned Course, i.e. summary of all plans in the Course.
Example Planned Phase, i.e. summary of all plans linked to the same prescription.
Example Radiotherapy Treated Phase, i.e. all treatments related to the same prescription.
Example Radiotherapy Treated Plan.
Example Radiotherapy Treatment Plan.
ReadOnly flag is set to true if Resource information is read only and can not be modified.
Duration prior to scheduled time before which a reminder should be notified.
Whether the resource can be scheduled or not.
Department(s) associated with resource.
Session dose limit
An individual's physical and/or emotional attraction to another individual of the same gender, opposite gender, or both genders.
Stage Category example for the *Varian ARIA API Stage Category Profile.*
Stage Group example for the *Varian ARIA API Stage Group Profile.*
Subcomponent of component. It contains only extensions, no value. It acts as a group placeholder for subcomponent attribute and its value in case of abnormal observation.
Task supporting information.

Task resource represents tasks configured in ARIA.
A task resource describes an activity that can be performed and tracks the state of completion of that activity. It is a representation that an activity should be or has been initiated, and eventually, represents the successful or unsuccessful completion of that activity.
Task example for the *Varian ARIA API Task Profile.*
Minutes Duration (Time Frame) estimate required to perform task.
Total dose limit
Sub-component attribute value.

* ValueSet specifies a set of codes drawn from one or more code systems, intended for use in a particular context. Value sets link between CodeSystem definitions and their use in coded elements.
* ^publisher = "Varian ARIA API Profile Team"
* Value sets have 2 aspects:
* .compose: A definition of which codes are intended to be in the value set ("intension") -/li- * .expansion: The list of codes that are actually in the value set under a given set of conditions ("extension") -/li-
* Please refer the How to -> Valueset & Codesystem List Page for list of valueset and codesystems.
Payor Plan Type. Applicable: PAYOR-INFO ValueSet.
Valueset Required or not flag.
vital-component example for the *Varian ARIA API vital-component Profile.*
